payload.claim_text: PSA's infrastructure improvements include construction of four new MNR stations in the Bronx, two new substations in the Bronx, and other right-of-way augmentations.
payload.description: List of infrastructure improvements under the Penn Station Access project

payload.claim_text: PSA will be a new MNR link to Penn Station that will provide one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers. The new MNR service will run through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, enter Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, and connect to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking, from which it will access Penn Station.
payload.description: Description of the Penn Station Access project scope and routing

records.display_name: Easement purpose for Van Nest substation

payload.claim_text: The above-described permanent subsurface easement is required for the new Van Nest AC substation in the Bronx to support the supplementary power demands associated with additional train traffic on the railroad right-of-way.
payload.description: Purpose of the permanent subsurface easement for the Van Nest AC substation

description: Project to bring Metro-North into Penn Station and construct four new rail stations in the Bronx. Design Build and PMC contracts awarded on December 29, 2021. Total project budget $12.867 billion.

Provide Metro-North Railroad New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, almost 20 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new and reconfigured interlockings, modernization of signal, power and communication infrastructure, and expansion of Metro-North's New Rochelle Yard.
New Metro-North Penn Station Access Project building four new stations in the East Bronx, creating shorter commutes for half a million people within a half mile of those stations

Construction of four new ADA compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Upon completion, serves as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station. Approximately 500,000 people live within one mile of the four new stations in Hunts Point, Parkchester/Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-Op City.
Construction of stations (including platforms, stairways, walkways, ADA compliance), electrical substations, and ROW augmentations along Amtrak's Hell Gate Line

Construction, maintenance, and operation of three overhead catenary structures at Concrete Plant Park, Bronx along Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes permanent acquisitions for stations, electrical substations, and ROW augmentations.

Project to provide new MNR rail service to and from Penn Station, including four new ADA stations in the Bronx, 19 miles of new track, over 200 new catenary structures, 8 new power substations, rehabilitation/replacement of 4 bridges.

Construction of four new ADA-compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, serving as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station, with stations in Hunts Point, Parkchester/Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-Op City.
Permit with the City of New York at Concrete Plant Park (Edgewater Road Right-of-way) for Penn Station Access Project

New Metro-North link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, and connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station. Includes construction of four new stations in the Bronx (Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, Co-op City), two new substations in the Bronx, two new substations in Queens (48th Street and Gate), and other right-of-way augmentations.
New Metro-North link to Penn Station that will provide one-seat passenger rail service to Penn Station for MNR's New Haven Line customers

PSA will be a new Metro-North link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station. Infrastructure improvements include construction of four new Metro-North stations in the Bronx and two new substations in Queens.

Enhances Metro-North service by providing a link directly to Penn Station on the New Haven line. Includes addition of 4 new ADA stations in the East Bronx as well as significant infrastructure enhancements.
Bringing Metro-North service into Penn with the addition of four new stations in the Bronx

Will provide Metro-North New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, 19 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, bridge rehabilitations, signal/communications upgrades, and expansion of New Rochelle Yard.
Additional funding for rolling stock

New Metro-North link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers. Runs through Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, enters Queens via Hell Gate Bridge, connects to LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking. Includes four new stations in Bronx (Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, Co-op City) and two new substations in Queens.

Provides new MNR rail service to and from Penn Station, delivering four new ADA stations in the Bronx, reducing commuting times by up to 50 minutes each way. 19 miles of new track, over 200 new catenary structures, 8 power substations, rehabilitation of 4 bridges. Budget $2.8B.
Capital program amendment on December 15, 2021 increased the 2020-2024 Capital Program by $535 to support the Penn Station Access project.

Master agreement with Amtrak to facilitate acquisition of permanent easements in the Bronx and Queens, and acquisition of permanent easements from HD Development of Maryland, Inc. at 50-02 Northern Boulevard, Queens in support of the Penn Station Access project
extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx
Easements in support of the Penn Station Access Project for the Co-op City Station and along Amtrak's Hell Gate Line
Acquisition of permanent easements from NYC Parks at Hutchinson River Parkway Extension in support of the Penn Station Access Project, including Co-op City Station western egress
Acquisition of permanent easements from NYC Parks and Recreation at the Hutchinson River Parkway Extension in support of the Penn Station Access Project in the Bronx.
Acquisition of permanent easements from NYC Parks and Recreation at the Hutchinson River Parkway Extension in support of the Penn Station Access Project, Bronx, NY
MTA project for Metro-North access to Amtrak right of way via Penn Station Access, with design-build contractor Halmar/Railworks (HRJV)
Expanding Access to Transit

MTA Capital Construction & Development project including pre-design, project management, track and structures, stations, engineering consulting, force account, design-build construction, arts at stations, program management, construction management, shops and yards construction, real estate management, controlled insurance, design-build construction services
Extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line (NHL) into Penn Station, along with four new accessible stations in the Bronx
Penn Station Access mentioned as a priority initiative for expense growth

Penn Station Access will provide Metro-North Railroad New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, 19 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, bridge rehabilitations, new interlockings, signal/power/communication modernization, and expansion of New Rochelle Yard.

Extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx. Hiring planned to start in 2026, a one-year delay from the February Plan, with service assumed to commence in 2028.

Will deliver four new accessible stations to the East Bronx, reduce commute times to Midtown by up to 50 minutes, and bring train service to within a mile of 500,000 additional residents. Early challenges with Amtrak caused delays, but new coordination strategies are helping. Next steps include completing schedule re-sequencing and creating cost and schedule efficiencies.

MTA C&D project to bring Metro-North Railroad service to Penn Station, with project management, track and structures, stations, engineering consulting, force account, design build construction, arts at stations, project reserve, construction management, shops and yards construction at New Rochelle Yard, real estate management, controlled insurance, design build construction services, fleet reserve, and construction reserve
New Metro-North route providing service from Penn Station to four new Bronx stations via Amtrak territory. Includes procurement of coach cars and dual-mode B+AC locomotives for rolling stock.
MTA Construction & Development continues to progress the Penn Station Access project, which will extend Metro-North New Haven Line service into Penn Station.

Construction of PSA requiring acquisition of real property interests for stations (including platforms, stairways, walkways and ADA compliance), electrical substations, right-of-way augmentations along Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, and New Rochelle Yard improvements

Acquisition of real property interests in connection with the Penn Station Access Project, for stations, electrical substations, ROW augmentations along Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, and New Rochelle Yard improvements
Authorization to acquire property interests by negotiated agreement or eminent domain in the Bronx and New Rochelle, for the Penn Station Access Project
Project to bring Metro-North Railroad service to Penn Station, includes New Rochelle Yard expansion

MTA MNR project providing direct rail access to Penn Station-New York from southern Connecticut, Westchester County, and the east Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, with four new Metro-North stations in the East Bronx at Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-op City. Property acquisition phase with September 2025 eminent domain proceedings.

Metro-North Penn Station Access will provide MNR New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, 19 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new interlockings, signal/power/communication modernization.
Advancing Penn Station Access project - Hell Gate Line in the Bronx, new Parkchester/Van Nest Station

Adding four new Metro-North stations in the East Bronx (Co-op City, Morris Park, Parkchester-Van Nest, and Hunts Point) by rebuilding Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Project includes track, signals, power, communication systems, bridges, and drainage. Projected to serve approximately 30,000 daily riders.

Full completion of the project, which extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx, originally scheduled for 2027, now expected to be delayed until 2030 due to coordination issues with Amtrak for work on its right-of-way. MTA planning to provide about 30 percent of original scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations as interim plan.
Penn Station Access project with $2,948.7 million budget in the 2020-2024 Capital Program
New Metro-North Railroad link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service to Penn Station for MNR's New Haven Line customers

Full completion of the project, which extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx, originally scheduled for 2027, now expected to be delayed until 2030. Interim plan to begin providing about 30 percent of planned scheduled service to three of four new Bronx stations in 2027.

A new MNR link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, and connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking. Infrastructure improvements include construction of four new MNR stations in the Bronx, two new substations in the Bronx, and other right-of-way augmentations.

New MNR link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, connecting to LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station. Includes construction of four new MNR stations in the Bronx, two new substations, and other right-of-way augmentations.

New MNR link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station. Includes construction of four new MNR stations and two new substations in the Bronx.
Acquisition of permanent easement and construction license from RLF Bassett II LLC for a pedestrian overpass at the south entrance of Metro-North Railroad's new Morris Park Station in the Bronx

New MNR link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station, including construction of four new MNR stations and two new substations in the Bronx
MTA Metro-North Railroad project to provide Metro-North service to Penn Station via the Amtrak Empire Connection, with related positions and financial adjustments
Extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx.

Extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station, creating four new accessible stations in the Bronx (Hunts Point, Parkchester, Morris Park and Co-Op City), improving existing tracks and bridges and cutting travel times from the Bronx to Manhattan.
MNR service expansion to Penn Station
Bring MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station by modernizing portions of Amtrak's railroad infrastructure on its Hell Gate Line

Acquisition of property interests along Erskine Place in the Bronx from the City of New York in support of the Penn Station Access Project, including acquisition of a sidewalk in support of the future Co-op City Station
Project to bring MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station, including modernizing Amtrak's Hell Gate Line infrastructure and constructing four new MNR passenger stations

MTA C&D is undertaking the Project to bring MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station. Modernizing Amtrak's railroad infrastructure on its Hell Gate Line by realigning tracks, optimizing overhead catenary systems, building electrical power systems, replacing and installing signal equipment, and constructing four new MNR passenger stations.
Board approved acquisition of property interests along Erskine Place in the Bronx from the City of New York in support of the Penn Station Access Project.

Bringing MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station, modernizing portions of Amtrak's Hell Gate Line infrastructure, building electrical power systems including substations, and constructing four new MNR passenger stations
Penn Station Access had 45 budgeted positions and 0 actual positions as of March 31, 2026, reflecting internal re-organization.

Acquisition of property along Bruckner Boulevard between Southern Boulevard and East 144th Street in the Bronx for construction, operation and maintenance of an electric power substation. MTA C&D is modernizing portions of Amtrak's Hell Gate Line by realigning tracks, optimizing overhead catenary systems, building electrical power systems inclusive of substations, replacing and installing signal equipment, and constructing four new MNR passenger stations.

New MTA Metro-North Railroad link to Penn Station traveling through Eastern Bronx and Queens via Amtrak's Hell Gate Bridge, providing one-seat passenger rail service to Penn Station for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers.
Metro-North Railroad Penn Station Access project connecting Penn Station to New Rochelle via Hell Gate Line

New MTA Metro-North Railroad link to Penn Station traveling through Eastern Bronx and Queens via Amtrak's Hell Gate Bridge providing one-seat passenger rail service to Penn Station for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers
Project to connect Metro-North customers to Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, building four new Metro-North stations at Co-op City, Parkchester, Morris Park and Hunts Point in the Bronx
New MNR link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for MNR's New Haven Line customers. Infrastructure includes four new MNR stations and two new substations in the Bronx.
Providing direct rail access to Penn Station-New York from southern Connecticut, Westchester County, and the east Bronx
4 new stations in the East Bronx, 20 miles of track, new substations and power systems, new interlockings, new signals, new comms systems
MTA project referenced in the Capital Program Committee meeting as an additional project for relocation services and as a major commitment achievement.
PSA project budget increase of $921 million from $1.561 billion to $2.482 billion across 2015-2019 and 2020-2024 programs

Design-Build Services to provide Metro-North customers with service into and out of Penn Station via the existing Amtrak Hell Gate Line, including construction of four new ADA accessible Metro-North stations along the Hell Gate Line in the East Bronx

Project to provide Metro-North Railroad customers with service into and out of Penn Station in New York via the existing Amtrak Hell Gate Line, including four new ADA accessible passenger rail stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, and over 19 miles of new and rehabilitated track work

Metro-North has been working hard on advancing Penn Station access, which will bring Metro-North New Haven line service to Penn Station and add four new stations to the East Bronx. This new service is a game changer for Bronx residents and for Metro-North customers in Westchester and Connecticut, who will have direct access to the West Side – saving up to 40 minutes per day.
Board approved two contracts: CBX-001 to Halmar International/RailWorks JV for $1,850,643,000 over 63 months; PS21008 to WSP USA/Atlas ATC Engineering JV for $116,572,815 over 86 months
Major capital project listed in MTA Board Update on Federal Funding

Provides Metro-North New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, almost 20 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new and reconfigured interlockings, modernization of signal, power and communication infrastructure.
MTA Expansion project to bring Metro-North service to Penn Station; design-build awarded Dec 2021; $385M added for New Rochelle Yard and $312M for initial fleet acquisition

Key equity project delivering dramatically improved transportation access to the East Bronx and providing direct access to Penn Station for riders on Metro-North's New Haven Line. Revised total $2.748 billion.
Amendment #2 adds funding for New Rochelle Yard and rolling stock portions with long lead-times
Support plans for Wayside, Onboard and Office updates required for Penn Station Access

Board approved Determination and Findings under the NY Eminent Domain Procedure Law for acquisition of fee interests and permanent easements in the Bronx, Queens, and Westchester for the Penn Station Access Project. Project provides direct rail access to Penn Station from southern Connecticut, Westchester, and East Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, with four new Metro-North stations at Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-op City. Estimated time frame for property needs: late 2023 through mid-2024.

Provide direct rail access to Penn Station-New York and Manhattan's west side from southern Connecticut, Westchester County, and the east Bronx. Infrastructure improvements to Amtrak's Hell Gate Line and construction of four new Metro-North stations at Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-op City.
